what_it_does: This is an npm lockfile (lockfileVersion 3) for the 'recollection' package. It records the exact resolved versions, tarball URLs, integrity hashes, transitive dependencies, platform-specific optional packages, engine requirements, bin entries, and peer-dependency metadata needed to reproduce the dependency tree on install.
observations: Project metadata: name 'recollection', version '1.4.2', license 'MIT', hasInstallScript true.
Direct dependencies include @anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk, @huggingface/transformers, @modelcontextprotocol/sdk, better-sqlite3, marked, proper-lockfile, sqlite-vec, and zod.
Dev dependencies include TypeScript, esbuild, tsx, vitest, and @types packages for Node, better-sqlite3, marked, and proper-lockfile.
Four CLI bin entries are declared: recollection, recollection-index, recollection-mcp-server, and recollection-search.

Resolved versions often differ from the requested semver ranges, e.g. claude-agent-sdk ^0.2.126 resolved to 0.2.141, esbuild ^0.25.11 resolved to 0.25.12, and @modelcontextprotocol/sdk ^1.20.0 resolved to 1.29.0.
The lockfile contains many optional, platform-specific native packages for claude-agent-sdk, esbuild, rollup, and sharp/libvips covering darwin/linux/win32 and arm64/x64/musl variants.
@modelcontextprotocol/sdk brings in a broad runtime surface including express, hono, cors, jose, raw-body, ajv, and zod-to-json-schema.
@huggingface/transformers depends on onnxruntime-node, onnxruntime-web, and sharp, indicating local ML inference with image preprocessing support.
Most packages include 'engines': { 'node': '>=18' } or similar, and integrity values are sha512 hashes from registry.npmjs.org.
standouts: Heavy reliance on optional, prebuilt native binaries across multiple architectures (Claude agent SDK, esbuild, sharp, rollup).
AI/LLM-centric dependency stack: Anthropic Claude agent SDK, Hugging Face Transformers, and Model Context Protocol SDK.
Local-first data stack: better-sqlite3 plus sqlite-vec for on-device vector search.
hasInstallScript true signals post-install native compilation or setup steps.
conventions: Follows npm lockfileVersion 3 schema with 'packages' and 'node_modules/*' keys.
Uses exact pinned versions, resolved npm registry URLs, and sha512 integrity hashes.
Marks dev dependencies with 'dev': true and optional platform packages with 'optional': true.
Records peerDependencies and peerDependenciesMeta, including optional peer flags.
concerns: hasInstallScript combined with many native optional dependencies increases install-time failure risk on unsupported platforms or missing build tools.
Optional platform-specific binaries may silently skip on unsupported OS/CPU, potentially causing runtime errors if the host code expects them.
Wide transitive dependency surface from MCP SDK and Hugging Face Transformers increases supply-chain exposure.
notes: A dense, AI-oriented npm lockfile that pins exact dependency versions and pulls in a large matrix of optional native binaries for cross-platform CLI/MCP tooling.
questions: What does the 'hasInstallScript' step actually execute, and does it build native code or only configure prebuilt binaries?
Why does the package request marked ^16.4.0 but no resolved entry for marked appears in the visible portion?
What is the license text referenced by 'SEE LICENSE IN README.md' / 'SEE LICENSE IN LICENSE.md' for the Anthropic packages?
apparent_themes: Local-first storage with SQLite and vector search
TypeScript-based CLI and MCP server development
intention_signals: The file is trying to freeze a complex, AI-native Node.js dependency graph so that the recollection CLI, indexer, search tool, and MCP server install and run deterministically across common operating systems and CPU architectures.
contribution: This file contributes an exact, integrity-verified dependency snapshot and a declared platform-native binary matrix, which a fused system would keep to preserve reproducible installs and supply-chain auditability.
summary_for_embedding: An npm lockfile for the 'recollection' v1.4.2 MIT package pinning exact versions, integrity hashes, and optional platform-specific native binaries for an AI/LLM CLI with MCP, SQLite/vector, and Hugging Face/Anthropic dependencies.
intention_for_embedding: To guarantee reproducible, cross-platform installation of a TypeScript AI memory/recollection toolset with local vector search and MCP server support.
